dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Task spec explicitly requires viewport {width: 375, height: 667} (iPhone SE). The playwright.config.ts uses projects[0].use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] }, which spreads viewport: {width: 1280, height: 720} and overrides the top-level viewport setting. All tests therefore run at 1280x720 — the mobile viewport requirement is unmet. The tap-target sanity check also runs at desktop resolution, not the required 375px mobile width.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Wrong-PIN error state is tested. seed-db.ts properly uses reject callbacks on IDB errors. Minor: the 300ms arbitrary waitForTimeout in parent-dashboard.spec.ts line 42 is a fragile wait that could mask state-management race conditions under load.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 4 user journey tests are present and the app-side fixes (tap targets, testids) are correctly applied. However, since the viewport override causes tests to run at 1280px, tap-target verification does not actually prove 375px mobile usability. Celebration overlay text check uses a loose truthiness assertion on potentially-null textContent.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Test structure is clean with a well-designed seedDB helper that computes PIN hash dynamically and uses declarative Playwright waits. Minor issue: page.waitForTimeout(300) in parent-dashboard.spec.ts is a synchronous sleep rather than a proper declarative wait (e.g. waitForFunction or waitForSelector on the cleared pin display), making the test brittle.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 4 critical journeys have tests. Sanity checks cover API keys, component size, routes, aria-labels, and tap targets. However the viewport misconfiguration means mobile-specific behavior (layout reflow, elements hidden at narrow widths, overflow) is entirely untested. The session test hardcodes 3 card-next-btn clicks, which is correct only because the session is hardcoded to 3 words — a comment explaining this coupling would prevent future regression.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded secrets in test code. PIN hash salt is sourced from the app's own crypto.ts value. The sanity test correctly distinguishes 'process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY' (allowed) from bare literal values (not allowed). Minor: the salt 'littlebridge-2026' is duplicated between seed-db.ts and src/lib/crypto.ts — a divergence would silently break PIN authentication in tests without a compile-time error.

issues.description: Project-level `use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] }` overrides the top-level `viewport: { width: 375, height: 667 }`. Playwright merges project-level use on top of global use, and devices['Desktop Chrome'] includes `viewport: { width: 1280, height: 720 }`. All 15 tests therefore execute at 1280×720 — the iPhone SE viewport requirement from the task spec is violated. The sanity test's getBoundingClientRect tap-target check also runs at desktop width, so it cannot verify that interactive elements meet the 88px minimum at 375px mobile width.
issues.fix_guidance: Replace `use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] }` with `use: { browserName: 'chromium' }` so the project inherits the global viewport of 375×667 without overriding it. The correct config block is: `projects: [{ name: 'chromium', use: { browserName: 'chromium' } }]`. Alternatively, keep the device spread but explicitly re-assert the viewport: `use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'], viewport: { width: 375, height: 667 } }`.

issues.description: Line 42 uses `await page.waitForTimeout(300)` — a fixed sleep to wait for PinPad digits state to clear after wrong PIN submission. This is a race-condition workaround rather than a deterministic wait, and it will break if the device/CI runner is slower than expected.
issues.fix_guidance: Replace the arbitrary sleep with a declarative Playwright wait that observes the actual UI state: e.g., `await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="pin-display"]')).toHaveText('')` or `await page.waitForFunction(() => document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="pin-dot-filled"]').length === 0)`. This ties the wait to real application state rather than elapsed time.

issues.description: The PIN salt 'littlebridge-2026' is duplicated verbatim from src/lib/crypto.ts. If the app salt is ever changed, tests will silently seed the wrong PIN hash, causing parent-dashboard and session tests to fail with no obvious error message pointing to the salt mismatch.
issues.fix_guidance: Either (a) import and re-export the SALT constant from src/lib/crypto.ts so both files share a single source of truth, or (b) add a comment in seed-db.ts explicitly cross-referencing `src/lib/crypto.ts:SALT` so the coupling is visible to future maintainers.

summary: The implementation correctly adds all 5 E2E test files, the seed-db helper, playwright config, and the required app-side fixes (missing data-testid attributes, tap target sizes, SongPlayer extraction). All structural requirements are met and the integration report shows passing tests. However, there is one P1 defect: the playwright.config.ts project definition uses `...devices['Desktop Chrome']` which overrides the required 375×667 iPhone SE viewport with 1280×720. This violates a hard task requirement and means the mobile UX is not being verified — tests pass at desktop resolution where layout, tap target measurement, and responsive behaviour may differ substantially from the 375px breakpoint the app is designed for. This must be fixed before the test suite can be considered a valid mobile quality gate.
